result.summary: Kraken exposes Spot REST, Spot WebSocket v1/v2, Unified FIX, a kraken CLI, and a built-in MCP server; OAuth is public-facing for client registration/authorization, and Spot REST uses HMAC-SHA512 API-Key/API-Sign auth while public market-data endpoints are unauthenticated.

kraken_oauth_client.label: Kraken OAuth client registration
kraken_oauth_client.setup: Register an OAuth client in Kraken’s OAuth client portal at [OAuth client registration](https://api.kraken.com/oauth/client). Use the resulting client credentials for OAuth flows; this is the easiest path for app authentication and may be tied to the scopes you request.

kraken_oauth_tokens.label: Kraken OAuth access token
kraken_oauth_tokens.setup: Authorize the app via Kraken’s OAuth authorize flow at [OAuth authorize](https://www.kraken.com/oauth/authorize), then exchange the code at [OAuth token](https://api.kraken.com/oauth/token) using the client registered in [OAuth client registration](https://api.kraken.com/oauth/client). Request only the scopes you need; known scopes include `mcp.base:read`, `mcp.portfolio:read`, `mcp.trades:read`, `mcp.trades:write`, `mcp.ledger:read`, and `mcp.funds:read`.
